rfi_text: The plans and specifications submitted do not demonstrate compliance with Building Code Clause E2 - External Moisture. Please provide documentation showing how the proposed cladding system will satisfy the performance requirements of E2, including manufacturer's installation details, BRANZ appraisal references, and any associated junction details.
expected_response: Updated cladding specification with E2/AS1 compliance pathway identified, BRANZ appraisal numbers, and manufacturer-specific installation details for all critical junctions.

rfi_text: The application proposes a cladding system outside the scope of E2/AS1 but does not identify it as an alternative solution. Please confirm whether you are relying on E2/AS1 or proposing an alternative solution. If alternative, please provide a design features report demonstrating how the cladding system meets the performance requirements of E2 - External Moisture.
expected_response: Design features report from the lead designer detailing the methodology for E2 compliance, including alternative solution justification and supporting evidence.

rfi_text: The site is located within a Flood Management Area on the Christchurch floor-level map. Site and floor levels must be specified in terms of the Christchurch Drainage Datum. The submitted plans reference levels in [datum/relative], which is not acceptable for this site. Please update all site and floor levels to Christchurch Drainage Datum.
expected_response: Revised site plan and elevations with all levels stated in Christchurch Drainage Datum, with reference to a verified survey benchmark.

rfi_text: The submitted foundation design does not appear to be coordinated with the recommendations from the geotechnical investigation report. Please provide written confirmation from the geotechnical engineer that the proposed foundation design complies with the recommendations of their report.
expected_response: Letter from geotechnical engineer (or annotation on geotech report) confirming the foundation design is consistent with their recommendations.

rfi_text: The application includes specific engineering design (SED) elements but does not confirm whether a Producer Statement for Construction Monitoring (PS4) will be supplied, nor does it include a schedule of construction monitoring. Please confirm PS4 provision and provide a monitoring schedule covering the SED elements.
expected_response: Schedule of construction monitoring + written confirmation that PS4 will be supplied at completion of construction monitoring.

rfi_text: The plans show barriers to upper floors and balconies that require specific engineering design. Supporting documentation for the barrier design has not been provided. Please supply full project-specific construction details for the barrier design, supported by a Producer Statement for Design (PS1).
expected_response: Engineered barrier details (drawings + calculations) with PS1 from a registered engineer.

rfi_text: Multiple essential items are missing from the application. Please refer to CCC's B-062 application checksheet and resubmit the following: [list]. We recommend submitting a completed B-062 with all future applications to avoid delays.
expected_response: Submission of all checksheet items + completed B-062 alongside the resubmitted application.

rfi_text: Planning issues have not been identified in this application. The proposed building work appears to materially affect District Plan compliance. We recommend you obtain a Project Information Memorandum (PIM) and/or arrange a pre-application meeting before we can progress this application. A section 37 notice may be issued.
expected_response: Either (a) evidence of resource consent already granted, or (b) confirmation from CCC Planning that the activity is permitted, or (c) revised plans removing the non-compliance.

rfi_text: The specification references a proprietary product/system that is relatively new in the New Zealand market. Insufficient supporting documentation has been provided. Please provide either test results to relevant standards or a recognised appraisal (e.g. BRANZ). If a CodeMark certificate is held, please provide the certificate of conformity and details of how the work meets the conditions and limitations of use.
expected_response: BRANZ appraisal, CodeMark certificate + conditions, or other recognised test/appraisal documentation.

rfi_text: The intended use of the building is not clearly defined in the application documentation. Building Code Clause A1 - Classified uses determines which Code clauses apply to the building work. Please clearly define the intended use(s) of the building and each space within it.
expected_response: A schedule on the plans (or in the design features report) listing each space and its A1 classified use category.

rfi_text: The application is for the first stage of a staged building project, but the manner in which construction is to be staged was not agreed with Council prior to lodgement. Please contact Council to agree on the staging approach. Note that full Code compliance may not be achievable until final fit-out is complete; staging documentation must address this.
expected_response: Staging plan agreed with CCC, identifying compliance milestones at each stage and how interim use will be managed.

rfi_text: The accessible route from the street boundary or accessible car park to the building's principal access has not been sufficiently detailed. Please provide sections through the accessible entrance and out to the footpath and kerb, showing finished surface levels and gradients to demonstrate compliance with D1 - Access routes.
expected_response: Detailed cross-section drawings showing the accessible route with gradients, levels at footpath/kerb interface, and identification of any 20mm change-in-level weathering strips.

rfi_text: The submitted plans show isolated steps on access routes. Building Code performance D1.3.3(i) states 'access routes shall not contain isolated steps.' This applies to all buildings other than detached dwellings, household units in multi-unit dwellings, outbuildings and ancillary buildings. Please remove the isolated step or provide a design that meets the acceptable solution requirements.
expected_response: Revised plans with isolated step removed, or replaced with a ramp/level threshold meeting D1/AS1.

rfi_text: There is a lack of coordination between the architectural drawings and the structural/civil/HVAC consultant drawings. The lead designer should reconcile inconsistencies before resubmission. Specifically: [items].
expected_response: Revised drawings reconciled across disciplines, with a designer's statement confirming coordination.

rfi_text: This is a new building with a floor area greater than 300m². Higher H1 insulation requirements apply. The application does not include a report demonstrating how adequate thermal resistance of the building envelope has been achieved. If you intend to use the modelling method, this report must be prepared by an appropriately qualified and experienced consultant.
expected_response: H1 envelope thermal resistance report (schedule method calculations or modelling method analysis) prepared by qualified consultant.

rfi_text: The combined floor area of all spaces in this commercial / communal non-residential building exceeds 300m². Compliance with H1.3.5 - Energy efficiency of artificial lighting is required. The application does not include energy efficiency calculations for the artificial lighting design. Please provide a statement from the lighting designer detailing the means of compliance (e.g. NZS 4243.2:2007 schedule method), with relevant calculations. Alternatively, include H1.3.5 in the scope of clauses covered by the electrical engineer's PS1.
expected_response: Lighting designer's statement + NZS 4243.2 calculations, or electrical engineer's PS1 explicitly covering H1.3.5.

rfi_text: The application does not justify the proposed number of sanitary facilities. G1/AS1 requires sanitary facilities for certain user groups. Please identify the user groups and the number of persons in each group for whom sanitary facilities are being provided. The MBIE toilet calculator is recommended.
expected_response: User-group schedule + MBIE toilet calculator output showing required vs proposed pans, basins, and urinals.

rfi_text: The fire safety design includes significant features but the application does not confirm fire engineer construction monitoring. Please engage the fire engineer for construction monitoring and include written confirmation with the application.
expected_response: Letter from fire engineer confirming the scope of construction monitoring and the deliverable PS4 at completion.

rfi_text: The application uses Verification Method C/VM2 as the means of compliance. Not all documentation agreed in the Fire Engineering Brief (FEB) process has been provided by the fire engineering designer and peer reviewer. Please refer to Engineering New Zealand Practice Note: Guidelines for Documenting Fire Safety Designs (2011) and supply the missing items.
expected_response: Complete C/VM2 design report, peer review report, FEB sign-off, and any annexed calculations per ENZ Practice Note.

rfi_text: Emergency lighting design documentation is incomplete. Please ensure emergency lighting drawings are supported by a Producer Statement for Design (PS1) and provide written confirmation that the emergency lighting designer will provide a PS4 on completion.
expected_response: Emergency lighting drawings + PS1 + PS4 confirmation letter.

rfi_text: There is no supporting design documentation for secondary or ancillary structural elements requiring specific engineering design (e.g. barriers, seismic bracing to building services, suspended ceiling systems, plant platforms). The structural engineer's PS1 covers only the primary structure. Please provide PS1s for all secondary/ancillary SED elements.
expected_response: PS1s + supporting calculations/details for each secondary SED element.

rfi_text: The proposed work involves a new water supply / increased wastewater outfall / change to stormwater outfall. Written approval from the relevant CCC network utility unit has not been included with the application. Please contact the appropriate team and submit their written approval: water capacity (WaterCapacity@ccc.govt.nz), wastewater capacity (WastewaterCapacity@ccc.govt.nz), or stormwater approvals (Stormwater.Approvals@ccc.govt.nz).
expected_response: Written approval email/letter from the relevant CCC team.

rfi_text: Mechanical ventilation is required for this building but mechanical drawings, specifications, and calculations have not been provided. Please engage a mechanical engineer to provide details, supported by a Producer Statement for Design (PS1). Note that the natural ventilation solution in G1/AS1 cannot be applied to occupied spaces in commercial/industrial buildings where products listed in G4.3.3 are generated.
expected_response: Mechanical drawings + specifications + calculations + PS1 from a mechanical engineer.

rfi_text: This application does not include a completed B-062 (residential) / B-063 (commercial) check sheet. CCC strongly recommends submitting a completed check sheet with every application to identify any missing items at lodgement.
expected_response: Completed B-062 or B-063 check sheet with all sections addressed.
source: CCC Forms and Guides + CCC Avoiding RFIs

rfi_text: The accessible car park is shown in a location that requires wheelchair users to travel behind other parked vehicles or share access ways with vehicles. Please relocate or redesign so wheelchair access avoids vehicle conflict. Where the accessible car park is not directly visible from the principal entrance, additional signage will be required.
expected_response: Revised site plan with accessible parking placement compliant with D1, plus signage schedule if not directly visible.

rfi_text: On QA review, the alternative solution proposed for E2 - External moisture interacts with fire-rated wall assemblies in a way that is not addressed in the design features report. Please clarify how the alternative cladding system maintains the required fire rating at the cladding/wall interface.
expected_response: Updated design features report addressing the E2/C interaction, supported by manufacturer documentation or fire engineer letter.
source: Synthesised from CCC Avoiding RFIs §commercial.weather_tightness + fire requirements

rfi_text: The site is in Technical Category 3 (TC3) per the MBIE Canterbury foundation guidance. The submitted foundation design relies on a standard TC2 solution, which is not appropriate for TC3 land. Please provide a site-specific geotechnical investigation and a specific engineering foundation design tolerating at least 300mm global lateral displacement (or the value specified for this site).
expected_response: Site-specific geotech report + SED foundation design + PS1.
source: MBIE Canterbury foundation guidance + CCC Avoiding RFIs §residential.structural_and_geotechnical

rfi_text: The wind zone classification used in the design has not been verified against the BRANZ MAP wind zone for this address. Please confirm the wind zone and provide documentation showing that the cladding and structural design align with the actual wind zone for the site.
expected_response: BRANZ MAP printout + revised specification confirming wind zone alignment.
